titlestmt.titleproper: Theresa Alt Tompkins County campaigns and elections collection8816
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Eirva Diamessis

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-11-20 00:50:08 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Theresa Alt Tompkins County campaigns and elections collection
did.abstract: Election and campaign files in Tompkins County, NY, including candidate literature, instructions, and rules, lists of volunteers, voters, and petitions, newspaper clippings, memoranda, correspondence, handwritten notes, annotated material related to candidates from Democratic Party, Working Families, and Green Parties; Democratic Party County, City and Ward Committee work, the Jesse Jackson presidential campaign (1988) and files from School Board elections (1986-2010), collected by Theresa Alt (b. 1943), document her political action and activism, dating from 1983-2010.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Theresa Alt Tompkins County Campaigns and elections collection documents the political action and activism of Theresa Alt (b. 1943), dating from 1982-2010. The collection contains election and campaign files in Tompkins County, NY, including candidate literature, instructions, and rules, lists of volunteers, voters and petitions, newspaper clippings, memoranda, correspondence, handwritten notes, annotated material, related primarily to Democratic Party candidates, as well as the Working Families Party, the Green Party, and a few opposition candidates. Election and campaign files are associated with the Democratic committees of Tompkins County, the City of Ithaca, and the Ward. The collection also includes campaign material from the Jesse Jackson presidential campaign (1988) and files from School Board elections (1986-2010).

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Theresa Alt Tompkins County campaigns and elections collection, #8816. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Theresa Alt (b. 1943) is a political activist located in Ithaca, NY, a long-time Tompkins County Workers Center (TCWC) Member Leader, Secretary of Ithaca Democratic Socialists of America, housing activist, and official Rental Housing Advisory Commission member. She grew up outside Washington, DC, and moved to Ithaca, NY, in 1976. She also lived in Poland, the Soviet Union, and Yugoslavia. She holds a Ph.D. in Slavic Linguistics from Columbia University. She was a Democratic candidate for the elections of the Commons Council (1994) and served as Mayor Nichols' administrative assistant for 5 years (1987). She hosted the WRFI Community Radio broadcast, "The Inquiring Socialist" (2020-2022), and took leadership in the progressive cable access show, DSA Presents.

did.unittitle: Series I. Campaign and Election Files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Loose newspaper clippings and literature.

did.unittitle: 48.496 voters Tompkins Board of Elections. Voter Export
Board of elections database for campaign use

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Documentation. Technological interest. Notes and printouts.

scopecontent.p: Theresa Alt was not active in this campaign.

scopecontent.p: Training materials and Theresa Alt's notes.

scopecontent.p: Earlier materials were collected to train petition gatherers in Ben Nichols' first mayoral race—several technicalities.

scopecontent.p: Older instructions from NYC; Tompkins County instructions for inspector in Fall Creek, Ithaca, NY.

scopecontent.p: Mostly Mike Dukakis' campaign literature.

scopecontent.p: Several map printouts. No materials from canvassing; turned them into the campaign.

did.unittitle: Voter registration project, 1983-84. Democratic Socialists of America

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Local Ithaca. Census statistics re voter registration. Includes handwritten notes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notes on who volunteered to register voters and how many we registered at Egan's IGA -- John Gutenberger-owned store on College Avenue.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: National League of Women Voters document.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Theresa Alt left the Democratic Party for a while and reregistered with the Working Families Party (WFP). Literature statewide and local, candidate literature, notes, lists, petitions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: County and 4th Ward Committee printed materials, Theresa Alt's notes, redrawing wards, Harkin campaign.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Older materials that had lasting value for an activist; 4th Ward and County Committee correspondence and notes from 1998-1999; candidate literature including a Green candidate Alt supported.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials concerning Theresa Alt's race for council -- notes, lists, literature, opposition literature, annotated voter lists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Record and pre-history.

scopecontent.p: Party newsletter and correspondence, meeting notes, annotated voter lists, party platform draft.
